rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Designated Defense Counsel'S Mo To Compel Pltf To Comply With General Order 140 And For Imposition Of Sanctions Against Pltf'S Counsel, And To Compel Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents And To Impose Sanctions Against Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, June 2, 2010 in Department 206 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Warren Pumps, LLC's Joinder's Berry and Berry's motion to compel Plaintiff to comply with General Order 140 and motion to compel further response to Demand for Production of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. General Order 140 requires Plaintiff to turn over pathology and related medical records to Berry and Berry. Berry and Berry has the authority to conduct discovery and bring motions to compel discovery. Berry and Berry's request to compel Plaintiff to sign a stipulation for a court order to release NPRC Military records is denied. The court lacks authority to compel parties to enter into stipulations. Plaintiffs' attorney of record, the law office of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP shall pay monetary sanctions of in the amount of $500 to Berry and Berry within 15 days. Sanctions are warranted because Plaintiffs' position with respect to Berry and Berry's authority to request pathology and bring this motion lacked substantial justification. =(220/JJM)
